title: Can't bind mount /etc/localtime
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sudo  podman run -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro debian ls -la /etc/localtime```


**Describe the results you received:**
`Error: open ..: No such file or directory: OCI not found`

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
$ podman run -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro debian:8 ls /etc/localtime
/etc/localtime

$ podman run -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro alpine  ls /etc/localtime
/etc/localtime

$ sudo docker run -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro debian ls -la /etc/localtime
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 27 Feb  8 00:00 /etc/localtime -> /usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc/UTC
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Can't reproduce locally. Can I ask where your Podman package came from - are you using the OBS repos?

I'm hitting this error message without doing anything particularly special with localtime. It seems to be something to do with reading timezone data, and it only happens in crun, not runc:

```
3767  syscall_0x1b5(0x6, 0x5620aeb43e20, 0x7fff22de0410, 0x18, 0, 0x28000000000007) = -1 EPERM (Operation not permitted)
3767  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9f4706545cb367707c0ddfb595ce954d7da08e2f68269292afddca9cee94f02e/merged/usr", 0x7fff22dde3b0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3767  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9f4706545cb367707c0ddfb595ce954d7da08e2f68269292afddca9cee94f02e/merged/usr/share", 0x7fff22dde3b0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3767  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9f4706545cb367707c0ddfb595ce954d7da08e2f68269292afddca9cee94f02e/merged/usr/share/zoneinfo", 0x7fff22dde3b0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3767  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9f4706545cb367707c0ddfb595ce954d7da08e2f68269292afddca9cee94f02e/merged/usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc", 0x7fff22dde3b0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3767  openat(6, "usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC|O_PATH) = 11
3767  readlink("/proc/self/fd/11", "/var/lib/containers/storage/over"..., 4096) = 130
3767  close(10)                         = 0
3767  openat(11, "UTC", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC, 0700) = -1 ELOOP (Too many levels of symbolic links)
3767  readlinkat(6, "usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc/UTC", "../UCT", 512) = 6
3767  mkdirat(6, "..", 0755)            = -1 EEXIST (File exists)
3767  syscall_0x1b5(0x6, 0x5620aeb43e50, 0x7fff22de0380, 0x18, 0, 0x28000000000000) = -1 EPERM (Operation not permitted)
3767  openat(6, "", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC|O_PATH) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
3767  close(11)                         = 0
3718  <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 23, NULL, 0) = 0
3767  close(7 <unfinished ...>
3718  epoll_pwait(4,  <unfinished ...>
3767  <... close resumed>)              = 0
3718  <... epoll_pwait resumed>[], 128, 0, NULL, 140571972713560) = 0
3767  close(6 <unfinished ...>
3718  epoll_pwait(4,  <unfinished ...>
3767  <... close resumed>)              = 0
3767  write(8, "\1\0\0\0\2\0\0\0open `..`\0", 18 <unfinished ...>
```
I'm running within an nspawn container, which makes me wonder if the syscall_0x1b5 returning EPERM is the problem again? I think 0x1b5 is openat2() ..

OK, openat2() problem is red herring, it still occurs with the syscall filtering fixed:

```
3167  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/8607c1f8097a0fe4dca381c9c454d69e8320722f140e6b56a1de16b1c79f2732/merged/usr", 0x7ffec51e5fe0, 4095) = -1  EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3167  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/8607c1f8097a0fe4dca381c9c454d69e8320722f140e6b56a1de16b1c79f2732/merged/usr/share", 0x7ffec51e5fe0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3167  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/8607c1f8097a0fe4dca381c9c454d69e8320722f140e6b56a1de16b1c79f2732/merged/usr/share/zoneinfo", 0x7ffec51e5fe0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3167  readlink("/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/8607c1f8097a0fe4dca381c9c454d69e8320722f140e6b56a1de16b1c79f2732/merged/usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc", 0x7ffec51e5fe0, 4095) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
3167  openat(6, "usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC|O_PATH) = 11
3167  readlink("/proc/self/fd/11", "/var/lib/containers/storage/over"..., 4096) = 130
3167  close(10)                         = 0
3167  openat(11, "UTC", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC, 0700) = -1 ELOOP (Too many levels of symbolic links)
3167  readlinkat(6, "usr/share/zoneinfo/Etc/UTC", "../UCT", 512) = 6
3167  mkdirat(6, "..", 0755)            = -1 EEXIST (File exists)
3167  openat(6, "", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC|O_PATH) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
3167  close(11)                         = 0
3167  close(7)                          = 0
3167  close(6)                          = 0
3167  write(8, "\1\0\0\0\2\0\0\0open `..`\0", 18) = 18

```
Just realised the version of crun in the kubic PPA is still 0.17; manually downloading the 0.18 binary fixes this issue..

Thanks for checking, @srd424!

crun 0.18 is just three days old :) Expect it to land soon in the Kubic repos.  @lsm5 PTAL

You might also want to use `--tz local` on the command line, or if you want all of your containers to run with the local timezone, use containers.conf.
```
man podman run
...
   --tz=timezone
       Set  timezone  in  container. This flag takes area-based timezones, GMT
       time, as well as local, which sets the timezone  in  the  container  to
       match  the  host machine. See /usr/share/zoneinfo/ for valid timezones.
       Remote connections use local containers.conf for defaults
```

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/tick-tock-container-time
